name: Bloat (Ruminal Tympany)
description: Accumulation of gas in the rumen that cannot be eructated. Can be frothy bloat (legume pasture) or free gas bloat (obstruction). Can be fatal within hours.
clinical_signs: distended left abdomen (rumen visible from behind)
complete cessation of rumination stops eating
cyanosis blue membranes in severe cases
vision_markers: visibly distended left flank (apple-shaped from behind)
drum-like sound on left flank percussion

diagnostic_criteria.definitive: Distended rumen on palpation/percussion. Stomach tube releases gas.

treatment_info.veterinary_treatment: Stomach tube to release gas. Anti-foaming agents (poloxalene or simethicone) for frothy bloat. Trochar in life-threatening cases – vet only.
supportive_care: Walk animal slowly to stimulate belching
treatment_info.first_aid: Walk cow slowly. Keep head higher than body. Call vet URGENTLY. Do NOT leave unattended.
treatment_info.do_not: Do not let animal lie flat. Do not drench if unconscious.
